data.title: FPT Software in Vietnam achieves ISO/IEC 42001:2023 AI Management System Accreditation, 2nd in SEA, emphasizing fair AI practices.
data.description: In Hanoi, Vietnam, FPT, a leading IT services company, has obtained ISO/IEC 42001:2023, the inaugural worldwide standard for Artificial Intelligence (AI) Management Systems. This accomplishment positions FPT Software as the pioneer in Vietnam and the second in Southeast Asia to receive this acknowledgment. The certification highlights FPT's dedication to ethical AI advancement and implementation, emphasizing fairness, impartiality, privacy, security, safety, quality, and robustness in their processes.

data.title: X-Sense opens German branch office in Langen, Hesse, near Frankfurt Airport & Central Station to enhance fire safety in Europe.
data.description: X-Sense, a renowned company in smart home and fire safety technology, has recently established its German subsidiary, X-Sense DACH GmbH, in Langen, Hesse, located at Monzastraße 2B. Situated close to Frankfurt Airport and Central Station, this new office serves as a strategic hub for the company's operations. It marks an important milestone in the company's global expansion efforts.

data.title: Equinor ASA announces fourth tranche of 2025 share buy-back program. (char limit: 74)
data.description: Below are details regarding transactions conducted as part of the fourth tranche of Equinor ASA's 2025 share buy-back program. Equinor ASA, listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange under the ticker OSE:EQNR, on the New York Stock Exchange under NYSE:EQNR, on the CEUX under CEUX:EQNRO, and on the TQEX under TQEX:EQNRO, engaged in the buy-back program. The transactions involved a significant number of shares, indicating a substantial buy-back effort by Equinor ASA as part of their ongoing share repurchase initiative.

data.title: Andrius Šemeškevičius appointed as CEO of LITGRID AB for a five-year term starting February 23, 2026.
data.description: Litgrid AB, with a legal entity code of 302564383 and registered office at Karlo Gustavo Emilio Manerheimo str. 8, Vilnius, LT-05131 Vilnius, Republic of Lithuania, announced the appointment of Andrius Šemeškevičius as the new CEO. The decision, made on January 12, 2026, came after a thorough assessment of the public recruitment process and the candidate's qualifications. Šemeškevičius is set to assume the role on February 23, 2026, for a five-year term.

data.title: BTC resilient as Powell mentions potential DOJ probe, reaching $92,000. Will price hold?
data.description: The price of Bitcoin surged to $92,000 following comments from Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell regarding a possible Department of Justice investigation into the Fed. The cryptocurrency's strong performance has raised questions about whether this momentum will be sustained in the near future.

data.title: Orion Resource Partners and SNB Capital collaborate to boost Saudi Arabia's mining sector, ensuring access to vital commodities.
data.description: In Riyadh, Saudi Arabia on January 13, 2026, a significant collaboration was announced between Orion Resource Partners LP, a prominent global investment firm focusing on mining and metals, and SNB Capital Company, the top capital market institution in Saudi Arabia and a subsidiary of the Saudi National Bank. This partnership highlights a strategic alliance between two key players in the industry.

data.title: RepRisk appoints new executives to drive growth as top data firm trusted by banks and asset managers. Headquarters in Zurich.
data.description: A leading data-as-a-service company, RepRisk, known for providing business conduct data to major banks and asset managers worldwide, has announced two new executive hires aimed at fostering company expansion. The appointments were made in Zurich on Jan. 13, 2026. RepRisk is recognized as a frontrunner in its field, offering crucial insights on reputational risks to its clientele.

data.title: Hunting PLC reports c.$135 million EBITDA, up 7% from 2024, with sales order book reaching c.$350 million in 2025.
data.description: In London, Hunting PLC (LSE: HTG), a global precision engineering group, released its 2025 Full Year Trading Statement for the 12 months ended on 31 December 2025, ahead of the Group’s audited full year results to be disclosed on Thursday 5 March 2026. The 2025 Summary Financials (unaudited) reveal a Group EBITDA of approximately $135 million, marking a 7% increase year-on-year. The Group also achieved a Group EBITDA margin of about 13%, compared to 12% in the previous year. A sales order book of around $350 million was noted at the end of the year.

data.title: CFTC creates innovation team for crypto, AI laws. Tyler Winklevoss and Kris Marszalek among key executives invited to lead guidance.
data.description: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is set to seek guidance from prominent figures in the cryptocurrency industry, such as Tyler Winklevoss and Kris Marszalek. They aim to consult with these key executives to provide insights on cutting-edge technologies.

data.title: Economist cautioning Trump's $200B housing stimulus will raise prices, not restore affordability due to increased demand.
data.description: Moody's Chief Economist, Mark Zandi, warns that President Trump's directive for Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac to purchase $200 billion in mortgage bonds may backfire by driving home prices higher instead of solving the nation's housing affordability problem. Despite Trump's claims that this move would restore affordability and the American Dream, Zandi argues that it ignores basic economics. Following the announcement, fixed mortgage rates decreased by 10-20 basis points to just over 6%, but Zandi cautions that this relief is illusory. While lower rates may support housing demand, the severe housing shortage will likely result in higher house prices. Zandi suggests that the influx of cash will bid up the cost of limited inventory, negating the benefits of slightly lower rates, making homebuying less affordable.
